text:
A kid named Pete woke up and looked around his room to make sure it was still the same. He ran downstairs to see Mom bustling about, serving breakfast without looking at him. "Mom?" he called out. There was no answer. After breakfast, he headed outside to meet Dad, who seemed preoccupied with a computer screen.

Pete: "Hey, Dad."

Dad: "Mmmm…"

A few clicks later and he sighed. "I'm working late again. It's fine."

Pete: "But what about me?"

Dad: "Time flies when you're busy. You'll understand later."

As they drove to school, Pete daydreamed about his future. He imagined himself hiking treacherous terrain, finding ancient ruins, solving mysteries. That day in science class seemed insignificant in comparison.

At school, the teacher droned on. Pete fidgeted. His mind was somewhere else. Then it hit him like a ton of bricks—the looming summer break.
